schema.description: A server for retrieving trending repositories and developers from GitHub, with filtering options by programming language, time period, and spoken language.

tools.name: get_github_trending_repositories
tools.description: Retrieves a list of trending repositories from GitHub based on specified filters such as programming language, time period, and spoken language.

language.description: Programming language to filter trending items by. Must be lowercase letters only, no spaces or special characters (e.g., "python", "javascript", "cpp", "csharp", "go", "rust", "java").

period.description: Time period for trending calculation. Valid values are "daily", "weekly", or "monthly".

spoken_language.description: Spoken language to filter repositories by. Must be an ISO 639-1 two-letter language code in lowercase (e.g., "en", "zh", "ja", "es", "fr", "de", "ko").

limit.description: Maximum number of trending repositories to return.

repositories.description: List of trending GitHub repositories matching the specified filters.

rank.description: The repository's position in the trending list.

name.description: The short name of the repository.

fullname.description: The full name of the repository in the format "owner/repo".

url.description: The URL to the repository on GitHub.

description.description: A brief description of the repository's purpose and functionality.

language.description: The primary programming language used in the repository.

stars.description: The total number of stars the repository has received.

forks.description: The total number of times the repository has been forked.

current_period_stars.description: The number of stars gained during the specified trending period.

tools.name: get_github_trending_developers
tools.description: Retrieves a list of trending developers from GitHub based on specified filters such as programming language and time period.

language.description: Programming language to filter trending items by. Must be lowercase letters only, no spaces or special characters (e.g., "python", "javascript", "cpp", "csharp", "go", "rust", "java").

period.description: Time period for trending calculation. Valid values are "daily", "weekly", or "monthly".

limit.description: Maximum number of trending developers to return.

developers.description: List of trending GitHub developers matching the specified filters.

rank.description: The developer's position in the trending list.

username.description: The GitHub username of the developer.

name.description: The display name of the developer.

url.description: The URL to the developer's GitHub profile.

avatar.description: The URL to the developer's avatar image.

repo.description: The featured repository associated with the trending developer.

name.description: The name of the developer's featured repository.

description.description: A brief description of the featured repository's purpose and functionality.

url.description: The URL to the featured repository on GitHub.

scenarios.description: Basic scenario with minimal trending data - one repository and one developer for daily Python trends

python_daily.name: awesome-python
python_daily.description: A curated list of awesome Python frameworks, libraries, software and resources

scenarios.description: Moderate scenario with multiple languages and time periods - testing weekly JavaScript and daily Rust trends with varied repository attributes

javascript_weekly.name: react
javascript_weekly.description: A declarative, efficient, and flexible JavaScript library for building user interfaces

rust_daily.name: rust
rust_daily.description: Empowering everyone to build reliable and efficient software

scenarios.description: Rich scenario with multiple programming languages, time periods, and comprehensive developer data - testing diverse trending patterns across different filters

python_monthly.name: transformers
python_monthly.description: State-of-the-art Machine Learning for PyTorch, TensorFlow, and JAX

python_monthly.name: django
python_monthly.description: The Web framework for perfectionists with deadlines

python_monthly.name: fastapi
python_monthly.description: FastAPI framework, high performance, easy to learn, fast to code, ready for production

go_weekly.name: docker
go_weekly.description: Moby Project - a collaborative project for the container ecosystem

scenarios.description: Edge case scenario with empty trending data and minimal optional fields - testing tool behavior with no cached results

test_cases.description: Valid: Rust daily repositories with limit=2

save_scenario.notes: Saved state includes original scenario data plus cached API results. State properly maintains all repositories, developers, and configuration maps.

test_cases.note: Tool executed successfully and returned 50 repositories. However, returned generic mock data instead of scenario-specific data from python_monthly array.

test_cases.test_name: Valid - Get Go weekly repositories with limit
test_cases.note: Tool correctly applied limit parameter and returned 2 repositories.
Tool executed successfully and returned 50 repositories.
Tool correctly rejected invalid period value with appropriate error message.

test_cases.error: Limit must be a positive integer
test_cases.note: Tool correctly validated limit parameter and rejected negative value.
Tool executed successfully and returned 50 developers.
Tool correctly applied limit parameter and returned 3 developers.
Tool correctly rejected invalid period value with appropriate error message.

save_scenario.note: State was successfully saved and includes both original scenario data and cached API call results. The saved state contains: original repositories (python_monthly, go_weekly, typescript_daily, java_daily), original developers (monthly, daily_zh), cached repository queries (repos|language:python|period:monthly, repos|language:go|period:weekly, repos|language:typescript|period:daily), cached developer queries (devs|period:monthly), and all configuration maps (languageCodesMap, spokenLanguageCodesMap, periodOptionsMap).

errors.error_details: When limit=0 was passed to get_github_trending_developers, the tool executed successfully and returned 50 developers instead of rejecting the invalid input. The validation code checks 'if limit and (not isinstance(limit, int) or limit <= 0)' but this fails when limit=0 because 0 is falsy in Python, so the condition 'if limit' evaluates to False and the validation is skipped.
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: ValueError('Limit must be a positive integer') | Actual: Tool executed successfully and returned full list of developers
errors.root_cause: The validation logic uses 'if limit and ...' which treats 0 as falsy. The correct logic should be 'if limit is not None and ...' to properly validate when limit=0 is explicitly passed.

tool_executions.test_case: Valid case with language and period

tool_executions.error: Limit must be a positive integer (correctly rejected)
Period must be one of: ['daily', 'weekly', 'monthly'] (correctly rejected)
Tool accepted limit=0 but should have rejected it (validation requires limit > 0)

test_cases.notes: Successfully returned 5 JavaScript repositories for weekly period with English spoken language

test_cases.test_name: Valid case with no parameters (boundary test)
test_cases.notes: Successfully returned 50 repositories (default) with mixed languages (JavaScript, Go, Python)

test_cases.test_name: Valid case with language and period filters
test_cases.notes: Successfully returned 50 Python repositories for daily period
Correctly rejected invalid period value with appropriate error message

test_cases.error: Limit must be a positive integer
test_cases.notes: Correctly rejected negative limit with appropriate error message

test_cases.test_name: Valid case with no parameters (boundary test)
test_cases.notes: Successfully returned 50 developers (default) with mixed language projects
Successfully returned 3 Rust developers for monthly period
Correctly rejected invalid period value with appropriate error message

test_cases.error: Limit must be a positive integer
test_cases.notes: Correctly rejected zero limit with appropriate error message

save_scenario.notes: Saved scenario contains all expected data structures with cached results from tool executions
